title: Urinary Retention - Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
chief_complaint: 68-year-old male with progressive difficulty urinating: hesitancy, weak stream, nocturia x4, and feeling of incomplete emptying for 1 year. Today unable to urinate for 12 hours with suprapubic pain.

physical_exam.abdomen: Distended bladder palpable to umbilicus, dull to percussion, tender
physical_exam.rectal: Enlarged prostate 60g, smooth, no nodules, no induration

hidden_data.psa: PSA: 3.2 ng/mL (slightly elevated, consistent with BPH)
hidden_data.us_bladder: Distended bladder, post-void residual 450mL, bilateral hydronephrosis mild

correct_diagnosis: Acute Urinary Retention due to Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
key_learning_points: PSA monitoring for prostate cancer screening

patient_responses.urination: I haven't been able to pee since last night
patient_responses.stream: My stream has been weak for a year
patient_responses.nocturia: I get up 4 times at night

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Acute retention from BPH.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is BPH with acute retention. Catheterize and start medical therapy.
feedback.attending_note: BPH with retention: Urethral catheterization (16-18F). Start tamsulosin 0.4mg daily. Trial of void after 3-7 days. If fails, TURP. Finasteride 5mg daily for prostates >40g. Monitor PSA annually.
